description_long: This is a cylindrical stone water well with a robust, stacked-stone wall forming the main body. The well is topped with a pitched, wooden roof supported by two vertical wooden posts, providing shelter from above. The roof is constructed from wooden planks, some of which are fastened with visible metal bolts or rivets, giving it a rustic appearance. The interior of the well is open and dark, suggesting depth and the presence of air or water inside. There are no visible mechanisms for drawing water, such as a pulley or bucket, and the well lacks any decorative embellishments. The structure appears sturdy and utilitarian, suitable for outdoor urban or park environments.
description: A cylindrical stone water well with a pitched wooden roof supported by two posts, featuring visible metal fasteners and an open, unadorned design.
description_view_0: Front view showing the flat wooden roof with metal fasteners and the open stone well beneath, with the supporting posts clearly visible.
description_view_1: Angled view highlighting the triangular shape of the roof and the circular stone wall, with one supporting post centered.
description_view_2: Rear view similar to the front, showing the wooden roof and open well, but with less visible detail on the fasteners.
description_view_3: Angled rear view emphasizing the pitched roof and the stone wall, with the supporting post visible in the center.
attribute_well_cover_design: Pitched wooden roof made of planks with visible metal fasteners; no lid or slab directly covering the well opening.
attribute_surrounding_protective_structu: Circular stone wall approximately waist-high, forming the main protective barrier around the well opening.
